The scene scale estimation network has 3 variants: G2H+SF, G2H+CamH, and CamParams. Model CamParams is an implementation version of single image camera calibration method with the addition of the camera height estimation head.
The scene scale estimation network has 3 variants: G2H+SF, G2H+CamH, and CamParams, each serving a different purpose.
Model G2H+SF only predicts local fields for ground2horizon and the scale field.
Model G2H+CamH is a mixture of predicting both local fields and global parameters.
Model CamParams is an implementation version of single image camera calibration method with the addition of the camera height estimation head.
All three models are able to construct ground2horizon and the scale field, which will be used in quantitative evaluations.
All the models have the same network architecture of PVTv2, a transformer-based feature extractor.
The decoder architecture for 2D field estimation follows the design of UNet that outputs the same resolution as the input image.
The number of channels in the decoder architecture differs according to the target output value.
The estimation head for the global parameter is a fully connected (FC) layer, following the design of previous work.
For the encoder, the PVTv2-b3 variant was utilized, which was pre-trained on ImageNet-1K.
